free_text: Fission fragment mass and energy distributions
for the neutron induced fission of Pu-239 as
functions of the resonance spins
Experimental site, H.W.,R.B.
C.W., G.W.-P. Experimental site.
C.W.
Double energy measurement.
Mass and momentum conservation relations and the
Shmitt-Neiler calibration are used to convert the
pulse heights to energies. The nu-bar versus
fission fragment mass curve was used to obtain
the pre-neutron masses and energies.
Two large Si(Au) surface barrier detectors,
which were cooled at a constant temperature to
obtain a good long-term stability of the measuring
chains.
The average masses and energies do no vary
much with the resonance energy or spin.
However is statement is subject to precaution
for the case of the spin, since several Pu-239
spin assignments remain questionable.
The 6.5 MeV additional excitation in the
case Pu-239 + N leads only to 1 MeV additional
fragment energy compared to the system Pu-240 s.f.
G.C.
Date is corrected
Last checking has been done.
SD: Cosmetic changes in all Subents.
Ref. P,INDC(EUR)-11,31,1977 added. Several Subents were
Pu-F(3), 98.35% enriched, 93 ug/cm**2, 20 mm
diameter evaporated onto a 20 ug/cm**2 thick
poly-imide backing. The total mass of Pu was 292 ug.
Since the nu-bar versus mass curve was not
known, it was deduced from the curve for
Pu-239, taking into account the respective
total nu-bar values. This is justified since
for all fissioning isotopes the nu-bar versus
mass distributions are very similar.
Table II of 79JUELICH,2,143,1979
Contains the statistical error and
the error from the fit.
Statistical error

updated. Data from Subents 003, 004 added.

Since the nu-bar versus mass curve was not

Pu-239, taking into account the respective
total nu-bar values. This is justified since
for all fissioning isotopes the nu-bar versus
mass distributions are very similar.

Mean total kinetic energy for 239Pu(n,f) resonances
GELINA.
Pu-O(2), 96.023% enriched, 40 ug/cm**2, 60 mm
diameter sprayed onto a VYNS backing of 50 ug/cm**2
thickness. The total mass of the Pu was 1.13 mg.
An additional run was done with a Pu-F(3) sample.
It was 99.956% enriched, 65 ug/cm**2 with a
diameter of 20 mm, and was prepared by evaporation
onto a 20 ug/cm**2 thick poly-imide backing.
The total mass of the Pu was 204 ug.
Poorly separated resonances
the spin of the dominating resonance is taken into
account.
Contains also the 14.68 eV resonance.
